ranges.label: Low Aggression
ranges.description: Your overall aggression score is low, indicating that you generally manage anger and interpersonal conflict effectively. You are unlikely to resort to aggressive responses in most situations and tend to approach disagreements calmly and constructively.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your effective emotional regulation and conflict resolution skills, as these are valuable personal strengths
Your low aggression contributes positively to your relationships and social environment; stay mindful of preserving these patterns
Consider sharing your coping strategies with others who may benefit from learning constructive approaches to frustration

ranges.label: Moderate Aggression
ranges.description: Your aggression level falls within the average range for most adults. You may occasionally experience frustration or engage in arguments, but these responses are generally proportionate to the situation and do not typically cause significant interpersonal problems.
suggestions: Review your subscale scores to identify specific aggression patterns, such as whether anger, hostility, or verbal aggression is most prominent
Practice active listening and assertive communication techniques to express your needs without escalating conflicts
Develop a personal toolkit of calming strategies such as deep breathing, counting to ten, or briefly stepping away from heated situations

ranges.label: Elevated Aggression
ranges.description: Your aggression score is elevated above the typical range. You may find it difficult to control aggressive impulses in certain situations, which could lead to conflict in relationships, work settings, or social interactions that affects your well-being.
suggestions: Learn and regularly practice structured anger management techniques such as cognitive restructuring and progressive muscle relaxation
Identify your personal triggers for aggression and develop specific coping plans for each common trigger situation
Consider enrolling in an anger management workshop or program where you can practice skills in a supportive group setting
Monitor how elevated aggression may be affecting your relationships and professional life, and seek feedback from trusted others

ranges.label: High Aggression
ranges.description: Your aggression level is very high and likely causing significant disruption in your interpersonal relationships, work performance, and overall social adjustment. Aggressive behavior at this intensity carries risks for both yourself and those around you.
suggestions: Seeking professional psychological help from a therapist experienced in aggression and anger management is strongly recommended
Cognitive-behavioral therapy has strong evidence for reducing aggressive thoughts, hostile attributions, and aggressive behavior patterns
Actively avoid high-conflict environments and develop a safety plan for situations where you feel your aggression may escalate
Be aware that chronic high aggression is associated with legal consequences, relationship breakdown, and physical health problems
Consider whether substance use or underlying mental health conditions may be contributing to your elevated aggression levels

physical.label: Low
physical.description: Your physical aggression score is low, indicating that you rarely consider or engage in physically aggressive acts. You are able to manage provocation without resorting to physical confrontation.

physical.label: Moderate
physical.description: Your physical aggression is at a moderate level. While you may occasionally feel the urge to respond physically when provoked, you generally maintain control over these impulses in most situations.

physical.label: High
physical.description: Your physical aggression score is elevated, suggesting a notable tendency toward physical confrontation when provoked. This level may lead to situations that risk injury to yourself or others and warrant attention.

physical.label: Very High
physical.description: Your physical aggression is very high, indicating frequent readiness to use physical force in conflicts. This poses serious risks for personal safety, legal consequences, and relationship stability.

verbal.label: Low
verbal.description: Your verbal aggression score is low, indicating that you generally communicate disagreements without resorting to arguments, harsh words, or verbal confrontation. You tend to express yourself calmly.

verbal.label: Moderate
verbal.description: Your verbal aggression is at a moderate level. You may sometimes get drawn into arguments or express disagreement in a forceful manner, but this does not dominate your communication style.

verbal.label: High
verbal.description: Your verbal aggression is elevated, suggesting a pattern of argumentativeness and confrontational communication. This may create tension in relationships and reduce the effectiveness of your communication.

verbal.label: Very High
verbal.description: Your verbal aggression is very high, indicating frequent and intense verbal confrontations. This level of verbal aggression can significantly damage relationships and create a hostile interpersonal environment.

anger.label: Low
anger.description: Your anger score is low, indicating that you rarely experience intense irritability or difficulty controlling your temper. You are generally able to remain calm even in frustrating situations.

anger.label: Moderate
anger.description: Your anger level is moderate. You may experience occasional irritability or temper flare-ups, but these are generally manageable and do not persist or escalate to problematic levels.

anger.label: High
anger.description: Your anger score is elevated, suggesting frequent irritability and difficulty controlling your temper. You may often feel like a powder keg, and this emotional volatility can affect your daily functioning.

anger.label: Very High
anger.description: Your anger level is very high, indicating persistent and intense anger that is difficult to control. This degree of anger significantly impairs emotional well-being and may lead to impulsive actions with serious consequences.

hostility.label: Low
hostility.description: Your hostility score is low, indicating that you generally view others with trust and do not harbor feelings of resentment, suspicion, or perceived unfairness. You maintain a positive outlook toward others.

hostility.label: Moderate
hostility.description: Your hostility level is moderate. You may sometimes feel that life has treated you unfairly or question the motives of others, but these thoughts do not dominate your worldview or relationships.

hostility.label: High
hostility.description: Your hostility score is elevated, suggesting a tendency toward cynical distrust, feelings of injustice, and jealousy. These hostile cognitions can erode relationships and contribute to chronic stress.

hostility.label: Very High
hostility.description: Your hostility is very high, indicating pervasive suspicion, resentment, and feelings of being treated unfairly. This cognitive pattern can lead to social isolation, chronic anger, and significant interpersonal difficulties.

ranges.label: Non-perfectionist
ranges.description: Your overall perfectionism score is low, suggesting that you do not place excessively high demands on yourself or experience significant distress about your performance. While some goal-setting can be beneficial, you may benefit from setting more structured personal standards.
suggestions: Consider whether setting slightly higher but achievable personal goals might help you grow and reach your potential more fully
Establishing specific, measurable objectives in areas that matter to you can provide motivation and a sense of accomplishment
Reflect on whether low standards reflect genuine contentment or possible disengagement from personal growth opportunities

ranges.label: Adaptive Perfectionist
ranges.description: Your score reflects adaptive perfectionism, characterized by high personal standards combined with a healthy ability to accept your performance. This balanced pattern is associated with achievement motivation, conscientiousness, and psychological well-being.
suggestions: Continue maintaining the healthy balance between striving for excellence and practicing self-acceptance when outcomes fall short
Stay vigilant for signs that your standards may be gradually becoming unrealistic or that self-criticism is increasing over time
Your adaptive perfectionism is a strength; channel it into meaningful goals while preserving flexibility and self-compassion

ranges.label: Maladaptive Perfectionist
ranges.description: Your score indicates maladaptive perfectionism, meaning you hold very high standards but frequently feel you cannot meet them. This pattern is associated with chronic self-criticism, anxiety, procrastination, and reduced satisfaction with accomplishments.
suggestions: Practice distinguishing between striving for excellence and demanding perfection; good enough is often genuinely sufficient
Develop self-compassion skills by treating yourself with the same kindness and understanding you would offer a friend
Focus on tracking your progress and effort rather than fixating on whether outcomes meet your ideal standards
Challenge all-or-nothing thinking patterns by recognizing that partial success and learning from mistakes are valuable outcomes
If perfectionism is significantly affecting your mental health or relationships, consider consulting a mental health professional

standards.label: Low Standards
standards.description: Your personal standards score is relatively low, indicating that you do not set particularly demanding expectations for yourself. While this may reduce performance-related stress, it could also limit your motivation to achieve your full potential.

standards.label: Moderate Standards
standards.description: Your personal standards are at a healthy moderate level. You set reasonable expectations for yourself that promote growth and achievement without creating excessive pressure or unrealistic demands on your performance.

standards.label: High Standards
standards.description: Your personal standards are very high, reflecting strong expectations for yourself across work and personal domains. High standards can drive achievement, but it is important to monitor whether they remain realistic and motivating rather than punishing.

discrepancy.label: Low Discrepancy
discrepancy.description: Your discrepancy score is low, indicating that you generally feel satisfied with your performance relative to your standards. This healthy pattern suggests you can accept imperfections and maintain self-esteem even when outcomes are not ideal.

discrepancy.label: Moderate Discrepancy
discrepancy.description: Your discrepancy score is moderate, suggesting that you sometimes feel your performance does not measure up to your expectations. Occasional self-criticism is normal, but persistent feelings of inadequacy may benefit from attention.

discrepancy.label: High Discrepancy
discrepancy.description: Your discrepancy score is high, indicating that you frequently perceive a painful gap between your standards and your actual performance. This chronic sense of falling short is strongly linked to anxiety, depression, and reduced life satisfaction.

ranges.label: Normal
ranges.description: Your pain catastrophizing score is low, indicating that you generally cope well with pain experiences without excessive worry, rumination, or feelings of helplessness. You are able to maintain perspective about pain and its likely outcomes.
suggestions: Continue maintaining your effective and balanced approach to coping with pain experiences when they arise
Seek appropriate medical care for pain management when needed, while maintaining confidence in your ability to handle discomfort
Your healthy pain coping style is a protective factor; staying physically active and engaged in life helps sustain this resilience

ranges.label: Mild Catastrophizing
ranges.description: You show some tendency toward pain catastrophizing, occasionally exaggerating the threat of pain or feeling unable to cope. While not severe, these patterns can amplify pain perception and may interfere with effective pain management strategies.
suggestions: Learn evidence-based pain management skills such as pacing activities, relaxation techniques, and cognitive coping strategies
Practice mindfulness meditation, which has strong research support for reducing pain catastrophizing and improving pain tolerance
Maintain regular moderate physical activity, as movement is one of the most effective strategies for managing pain and preventing deconditioning
If pain is persistent or worsening, consult a pain management specialist who can offer a comprehensive multimodal treatment approach

ranges.label: Moderate Catastrophizing
ranges.description: Your pain catastrophizing is moderately elevated, suggesting that you frequently ruminate about pain, magnify its threat, or feel helpless when experiencing it. This pattern is clinically significant and associated with increased chronic pain risk and slower recovery.
suggestions: Consulting a pain psychologist or mental health professional with expertise in chronic pain management is recommended at this level
Cognitive-behavioral therapy for pain has strong evidence for reducing catastrophizing, improving coping, and decreasing pain-related disability
Practice separating the physical sensation of pain from your thoughts and emotional reactions about it, as these are distinct processes
Discuss a comprehensive pain management plan with your healthcare team that addresses both physical and psychological components of pain
Gradually increase engagement in valued activities despite pain, as avoidance often worsens both catastrophizing and functional disability

ranges.label: Severe Catastrophizing
ranges.description: Your pain catastrophizing score is very high, indicating intense and persistent rumination, magnification, and helplessness related to pain. This level significantly amplifies pain perception and is strongly associated with disability, depression, and poor treatment outcomes.
suggestions: Seeking help from a pain psychologist or multidisciplinary pain clinic is strongly recommended, as catastrophizing at this level requires professional intervention
A comprehensive treatment approach combining psychological therapy, medical management, and physical rehabilitation is likely to be most effective
Avoid the cycle of excessive pain monitoring and rumination by scheduling structured activities and focusing attention on engaging tasks
Build and lean on a social support network, as isolation tends to worsen catastrophizing and pain-related suffering

helplessness.label: Low
helplessness.description: Your helplessness score is low, indicating that you generally feel capable of coping with pain and do not perceive it as an overwhelming or uncontrollable experience. You maintain a sense of agency when dealing with pain.

helplessness.label: Moderate
helplessness.description: You show some feelings of helplessness regarding pain, occasionally feeling that you cannot go on or that pain will never improve. These thoughts are present but do not yet dominate your pain experience.

helplessness.label: High
helplessness.description: Your helplessness score is elevated, suggesting frequent feelings that pain is unbearable and beyond your ability to cope. Learning active coping strategies and challenging helpless thinking patterns can meaningfully reduce this distress.

helplessness.label: Very High
helplessness.description: Your helplessness is very high, indicating a pervasive sense that pain is overwhelming and uncontrollable. This level of helplessness strongly predicts poor pain outcomes and warrants professional psychological intervention.

magnification.label: Low
magnification.description: Your magnification score is low, suggesting that you do not tend to exaggerate the seriousness or threat of pain. You are able to maintain a realistic perspective on pain sensations without catastrophic amplification.

magnification.label: Moderate
magnification.description: You show some tendency to magnify the threat of pain, occasionally worrying that something serious may happen or that pain will worsen. This moderate amplification can increase anxiety around pain episodes.

magnification.label: High
magnification.description: Your magnification score is elevated, indicating a strong tendency to amplify pain sensations and expect the worst possible outcomes. This pattern heightens fear and anxiety, which in turn can increase actual pain perception.

magnification.label: Very High
magnification.description: Your magnification is very high, reflecting an intense tendency to perceive pain as extremely threatening and expect catastrophic consequences. This amplification cycle significantly worsens the overall pain experience.

rumination.label: Low
rumination.description: Your rumination score is low, indicating that you are generally able to redirect your attention away from pain rather than dwelling on it. You do not become mentally preoccupied with pain sensations or their implications.

rumination.label: Moderate
rumination.description: You show some tendency toward pain rumination, occasionally finding it difficult to stop thinking about pain or wanting it to go away. These repetitive thoughts are present but do not fully consume your attention.

rumination.label: High
rumination.description: Your rumination score is elevated, suggesting that you frequently cannot stop thinking about pain, how much it hurts, or how much you want it to stop. This mental preoccupation intensifies suffering beyond the physical sensation itself.

rumination.label: Very High
rumination.description: Your rumination is very high, indicating persistent and intrusive preoccupation with pain that dominates your mental life. This intense cognitive fixation on pain significantly amplifies distress and impairs your ability to function.
